title: Prestwick Airport doubles weekly Air China Cargo flights - Air Cargo Week

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport (PIK) has significantly increased its China operations as carrier Air China Cargo doubled its weekly freighter services from four to

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Prestwick Airport doubles weekly Air China Cargo flights - Air Cargo Week • Glasgow Prestwick Airport has expanded its China connectivity as Air China Cargo doubled weekly freighter flights from four to eight, adding Chengdu to its existing Guangzhou route.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: • The move follows one of the busiest periods in the airport’s history, with three new cargo operators, major facility upgrades, and 200 new jobs created. • Prestwick expects continued growth into 2026 as it strengthens its position as a key UK–China cargo gateway, supported by new cool chain capabilities for seafood exports.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Prestwick Airport doubles weekly Air China Cargo flights
